# Rosalind Problem: Find All Occurrences of a Pattern in a String (Scala Solution)

## Problem Statement
Given two strings `pattern` and `text`, return all starting positions (0-based) where `pattern` occurs in `text`.

## Solution

```scala
def findAllOccurrences(pattern: String, text: String): List[Int] = {
  val occurrences = scala.collection.mutable.ListBuffer[Int]()
  
  // Iterate through text to find pattern occurrences
  for (i <- 0 to text.length - pattern.length) {
    if (text.substring(i, i + pattern.length) == pattern) {
      occurrences += i
    }
  }
  
  occurrences.toList
}

// Alternative more functional approach
def findAllOccurrencesFunctional(pattern: String, text: String): List[Int] = {
  (0 to text.length - pattern.length)
    .filter(i => text.substring(i, i + pattern.length) == pattern)
    .toList
}

// Most efficient approach using sliding window
def findAllOccurrencesEfficient(pattern: String, text: String): List[Int] = {
  (0 to text.length - pattern.length)
    .filter(i => text.slice(i, i + pattern.length) == pattern)
    .toList
}

// Example usage
val pattern = "ATAT"
val text = "GATATATGCATATACTT"

val result = findAllOccurrences(pattern, text)
println(result) // Output: List(1, 3, 9)

// Test with the sample dataset
val samplePattern = "CTAT"
val sampleText = "
